top_categories.workout_context: Krk Gran Fondo's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 51 miles.
51 miles on mixed terrain burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Krk Gran Fondo's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before April.
Krk Gran Fondo's mixed terrain rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
Base fitness for Krk Gran Fondo. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
At Krk Gran Fondo, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Krk Gran Fondo. Train it until it feels boring.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Krk Gran Fondo, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
2-minute all-out efforts on mixed terrain. Closing gaps, responding to attacks, sprinting for position. Train the burn.
Varied gradients on mixed terrain demand climbing versatility. Sit the shallow stuff, stand the steep stuff, switch without thinking.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.

race_overlay.heat: April in Island of Krk, Croatia can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before Krk Gran Fondo. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for Krk Gran Fondo’s 51 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Krk Gran Fondo. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Krk Gran Fondo requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Gravel Specific, Durability, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 51 miles of mixed terrain in Island of Krk, Croatia.
